name: Seabourn Sojourn
description: Seabourn Sojourn is the second of the Odyssey-class ships, delivered by T. Mariotti in Genoa in 2010. Like her sister vessels, she carries just 229 suites — every one with a private verandah — and a crew of 335 for 458 guests, producing the intimate yet attentive atmosphere Seabourn is celebrated for. Her smaller size allows access to boutique ports and tender-only destinations that larger ships simply cannot reach, making her a favourite for Northern Europe, the Norwegian fjords, and exotic itineraries across Asia and the Pacific.

dining: The Restaurant (main dining, open seating, no set times)
The Patio Grill (poolside outdoor grilled specialties)
The Club (cocktail bar with light bites)
entertainment: The Grand Salon (main entertainment, shows and performances)

name: Seabourn Quest
description: Seabourn Quest is the third and final Odyssey-class ship, delivered by T. Mariotti in 2011. She completed Seabourn's trio of intimate 458-guest yachts that redefined ultra-luxury small-ship cruising in the 2010s. With 229 ocean-view suites all featuring private verandahs and a nearly 1:1.4 crew-to-guest ratio, Quest exemplifies the Seabourn ethos: uncommon destinations, extraordinary service, and an onboard atmosphere that feels closer to a private yacht than a cruise ship. She is a popular choice for South America, the Caribbean, and far-flung expedition-style itineraries.

name: Seabourn Encore
description: Seabourn Encore debuted in January 2017, built by Fincantieri in Genoa as the first of the Encore-class ships.She expanded on the proven Odyssey-class design with larger suites, more dining venues, and the landmark addition of The Grill by Thomas Keller — a partnership with the celebrated Michelin-starred chef that transformed luxury dining at sea. Carrying just 604 guests with 450 crew, the ratio ensures the signature Seabourn service standard: staff outnumber guests. She was named by Dame Helen Mirren.

name: Seabourn Ovation
description: Seabourn Ovation is the sister ship to Seabourn Encore, delivered by T. Mariotti in April 2018. She carries the same elegant Encore-class design with 302 suites — every one with a private verandah — and the same world-class dining programme including The Grill by Thomas Keller. Named by Dame Marianne Faithfull, Ovation sails a wide range of itineraries from the Mediterranean and Northern Europe to Asia and Australia, embodying Seabourn's philosophy of ultra-luxury exploration.

name: Seabourn Venture
description: Seabourn Venture is the first purpose-built expedition ship in Seabourn's history, delivered by T. Mariotti in 2022. Designed to explore the world's most remote polar and expedition destinations, she carries just 264 guests with nearly a 1:1 crew-to-guest ratio. Her defining feature is two six-person Triton submarines — fully owned and operated by Seabourn and included in the expedition fare — enabling guests to dive beneath Antarctic ice shelves and witness underwater worlds few humans have ever seen. A fleet of 24 Zodiacs and expert expedition teams round out the extraordinary adventure.

entertainment: Expedition Lounge (daily briefings, lectures, and social hub)
recreation: Two Triton six-person submarines (included in fare)
Expedition walks and guided shore excursions with expert team

name: Seabourn Pursuit
description: Seabourn Pursuit is the sister ship to Seabourn Venture, delivered by T. Mariotti in 2023, completing Seabourn's ultra-luxury expedition fleet. Like her sister, she carries just 264 guests and is equipped with two Triton submarines, a full complement of Zodiacs, and a team of world-class expedition experts covering biology, glaciology, history, and photography. Her Polar Class 6 hull and dynamic positioning system allow her to venture into remote anchorages and wilderness areas inaccessible to conventional ships, while her interiors maintain the same Seabourn hallmarks of spacious suites, exceptional cuisine, and unmatched personal service.
